channel.title: Research Software Engineering on jamessmithies.org
channel.description: Recent content in Research Software Engineering on jamessmithies.org

item.title: Building Capability in RSE for the Humanities and Social Sciences (HASS)
item.description: Slides of my talk 'Building Capability in RSE for the Humanities and Social Sciences (HASS)' at RSAA25, with PT Sefton and Nick Jenkins.

item.title: Antipodal Experiments Digital Humanities & Social Science in Australasia
item.description: Slides of my talk 'Antipodal Experiments Digital Humanities & Social Science in Australasia’. D-Lab Berkeley, UC Berkeley, 19 May, 2025.

item.title: Australasian Research Software Engineering
item.description: Slides of my talk 'Australasian Research Software Engineering: From HASS Research Services to Infrastructural Milieux', presented at the Australasian Association of Digital Humanities (aaDH) seminar series, August 01, 2024.

item.title: HASS Research Software Engineering (RSE) in the UK
item.description: Slides of my talk 'HASS Research Software Engineering (RSE) in the UK', a contribution to the 'Bridging the Digital Divide in HASS Panel' held at the University of Melbourne, August 23, 2023.

item.title: Career Pathways in Arts & Humanities RSE in the UK and Beyond
item.description: Slides of my talk 'Career Pathways in Arts & Humanities RSE in the UK and Beyond '. Digital Humanities Research Software Engineering (RSE) Summer School, University of Cambridge / Zoom, 25 July 2023.

item.title: Book Launch: Collaborative Historical Research in the Age of Big Data
item.description: Video of the panel discussion for the launch of the open access book, Collaborative Historical Research in the Age of Big Data: Lessons from an interdisciplinary project (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 2023). The book is available via open access here. The roundtable discussion is led by hosts Professor Jane Winters and Professor James Smithies, with the authors, Professor Ruth Ahnert and Professor Emma Griffin. Unfortunately, Dr Mia Ridge and Dr Giorgia Tolfo were unable to join the discussion.

item.title: The Phenomenology of Technical Knowledge: Research Software Engineering, Product Development, and Digital Humanities
item.description: Slides of my talk 'The Phenomenology of Technical Knowledge: Research Software Engineering, Product Development, and Digital Humanities' presented via Zoom at the Bridges (Bridges between disciplines: Gender in STEM and Social Sciences) Conference, September 16th 2022.

item.title: Architecture and Research Software Engineering (Podcast)
item.description:
Contribution to a Code for Thought podcast, hosted by Peter Schmidt (UCL) and with Steve Crouch from the Software Sustainability Institute/Southampton (UK) and Keith Gutfreund from Elsevier, Boston (USA).

Click here for the podcast (30:47)

item.title: DHN2021 Archiving & Sustainability in King's Digital Lab
item.description: Slides of my talk 'Archiving & Sustainability in King's Digital Lab', presented at DHN2021 as part of the panel discussion 'Towards deterioration, disappearance or destruction? Discussing the critical issue of long-term sustainability of digital humanities projects', chaired by Mats Fridlund (University of Gothenburg) and with Jessica Parland-von Essen (Senior Coordinator at CSC– IT Center for Science, Finland) and Victoria Johansson (Deputy Director of Lund University Humanities Lab, Sweden).

item.title: MaDiH (مديح): Mapping Digital Heritage in Jordan: Update & Issues
item.description: Slides of my Zoom talks with Prof. Fadi Balaawi 'MaDiH (مديح): Mapping Digital Heritage in Jordan: A Brief Update', and 'Risks, Challenges & Opportunities' presented at our MaDiH (مديح) project workshop 'Identifying datasets on Jordanian Cultural Heritage: User feedback' on October 13th 2020, hosted by the Council for British Research in the Levant (CBRL).

item.title: King’s Digital Lab as Experiment and Lifecycle
item.description: This SORSE talk, delivered with KDL Deputy Director Arianna Ciula, describes King’s Digital Lab (KDL), a Research Software Engineering lab operating within the Faculty of Arts and Humanities at King’s College London (UK).  The KDL team of 18 project managers, analysts, designers, engineers, and systems managers specialise in arts & humanities, cultural heritage, and creative industries research and development. The talk provides a current state overview of the lab, and describes our RSE HR roles (see https://zenodo.org/record/2564790) and a relatively recent trial initiative that defines the different ways the team can contribute to research. A more technically-oriented overview of our ongoing work in refining the fundamental components of KDL Software Development Lifecycle (see https://github.com/kingsdigitallab/sdlc-for-rse/) is also provided, including descriptions of our modelling workflows, processes, and efforts to increase sustainability, reproducibility, and research data management best practice (https://zenodo.org/record/3361580#.X09x6mdKhhE).

item.title: King’s Digital Lab Research Software Engineering Career Paths
item.description: The slides of my talk 'King’s Digital Lab Research Software Engineering Career Paths', presented at the Russell Universities Group IT Directors Forum (RUGIT), 19 September 2019. See also Smithies, James. ‘Research Software (RS) Careers: Generic Learnings from King’s Digital Lab, King’s College London’. Zenodo, 7 February 2019. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.2564790.

item.title: Research Software Engineering in Jordan: The MaDiH (مديح) Project
item.description: Slides of 'Research Software Engineering in Jordan: The MaDiH (مديح) Project', delivered at RSE2019, 18 September, 2019.

item.title: Innovation at the Intersection: Using STS to enable University – Technology Sector Collaboration
item.description: Slides of my talk 'Innovation at the Intersection: Using STS to enable University – Technology Sector Collaboration', delivered at the 4S Society for the Social Studies of Science conference, New Orleans, 04 September 2019.

item.title: Integrating DH into the longue durée: Research Laboratories, History, Methods
item.description: My talk 'Integrating DH into the longue durée: Research Laboratories, History, Methods', presented at the Australian Academy of the Humanities’ 2nd Humanities, Arts and Culture Data Summit and 3rd international DARIAH Beyond Europe workshop, 27 - 29 March, 2019.

item.title: Research Software Careers: Establishing Local, National & International Pathways
item.description: Slides for my talk 'Research Software Careers: Establishing Local, National & International Pathways', at the launch of Research Software London, Imperial College London, October 18, 2018.

item.title: From Lab to University: Towards an Institutional RSE Career Pathway
item.description: Slides for my talk 'From Lab to University: Towards an Institutional RSE Career Pathway', delivered at Research Software Engineering 2018, Birmingham University, 03 September 2018.
